query: How do I configure 2D shadow softness in Godot?
model_answer: Use the Light2D's shadow_filter property; PCF5 and PCF13 are the two soft-shadow options. Set shadow_filter_smooth to control the blur radius.

query: What is a SubViewport and when should I use one?
model_answer: A SubViewport is a Viewport node that renders its child scene into a texture rather than directly to the screen. Use it for render-to-texture effects, split-screen, or in-game cameras that display on a surface.

query: How do I make a 3D character controller with jumping?
model_answer: Use CharacterBody3D with move_and_slide(). Set velocity.y for vertical movement and check is_on_floor() before applying jump impulse. Call move_and_slide() each physics frame.

query: How does Godot's CharacterBody2D differ from RigidBody2D?
model_answer: CharacterBody2D gives you direct control over velocity and collision response — you write the movement logic. RigidBody2D is driven by the physics engine with forces and impulses. Use CharacterBody2D for player characters; RigidBody2D for physically-simulated objects.

query: What is the recommended way to make a 2D top-down character move?
model_answer: Use CharacterBody2D with Input.get_vector() for 8-directional movement. Normalize the vector to prevent diagonal speed boost, multiply by speed, and call move_and_slide() each _physics_process frame.

query: Why is my CanvasModulate not affecting a specific layer?
model_answer: CanvasModulate only tints nodes on the same canvas layer or lower. Nodes on a higher-numbered CanvasLayer (e.g., UI layer at z=10) are composited on top and are unaffected. Move CanvasModulate to the correct layer or adjust CanvasLayer z_index values.

query: How do I export a scene to GLTF in Godot?
model_answer: Use the GLTFDocument and GLTFState APIs: create instances of both, call GLTFDocument.append_from_scene() passing your root node, then call GLTFDocument.write_to_filesystem() with the target .glb or .gltf path.

query: How do signals work in Godot and how do I connect them in code?
model_answer: Signals are type-safe callbacks. Connect with node.signal_name.connect(callable) or the editor's Node panel. Emit with signal_name.emit(). Custom signals are declared with the `signal` keyword at the top of a script.

query: How do I use AnimationPlayer to animate a sprite?
model_answer: Add an AnimationPlayer node, create a new Animation, then add tracks for the properties you want to animate (e.g., Sprite2D:frame). Insert keyframes at desired times and set play() on AnimationPlayer in code or via an AutoPlay setting.

query: What is the difference between _process and _physics_process?
model_answer: _process runs every rendered frame at variable intervals (delta varies). _physics_process runs at a fixed physics tick rate (default 60 Hz). Use _physics_process for physics, movement, and collision; _process for visual updates and input that doesn't affect physics.

query: How do I add a custom resource type in GDScript?
model_answer: Extend Resource in a GDScript file and use @export to expose properties. Load with load() or ResourceLoader.load(). Instances can be saved as .tres files using ResourceSaver.save().

query: How do I implement a state machine for enemy AI in Godot?
model_answer: Use an enum to define states and a match statement in _physics_process to dispatch logic. Transition by reassigning the current_state variable. For complex AI, each state can be a separate class extending a base State resource.

query: What is an Area2D node used for?
model_answer: Area2D detects overlaps with other physics bodies and areas without applying collision forces. Use it for damage zones, trigger regions, item pickups, and proximity detection. It emits body_entered/body_exited and area_entered/area_exited signals.

query: How do I save and load game data in Godot?
model_answer: Use FileAccess.open() with WRITE/READ flags. Store data as JSON with JSON.stringify() or as binary with PackedByteArray. For structured save files, store a Dictionary and serialize it. The user data directory (user://) is writable on all platforms.

query: Why does my scene instance look different in the editor vs running the game?
model_answer: Differences are usually caused by @tool scripts that only run in-editor, CanvasLayer offsets, or viewport stretch settings that differ at runtime. Enable @tool on the script if you need editor-preview fidelity. Check Project Settings > Display > Window > Stretch for runtime layout differences.

query: How do I play audio and control volume in Godot?
model_answer: Add an AudioStreamPlayer (2D or 3D for positional audio). Assign an AudioStream to the stream property and call play(). Volume is controlled via volume_db (decibels). Use AudioServer bus volumes for global mixing.

query: What is autoload and when should I use it?
model_answer: Autoloads are nodes or scripts loaded before any scene, available as global singletons. Use them for game state, audio managers, or event buses that need to persist across scene changes. Access them by the name set in Project Settings > Autoload.

query: How do I detect collision layers and masks correctly?
model_answer: A body's collision_layer defines which layers it occupies. collision_mask defines which layers it scans for collisions. Two bodies collide when body A's mask includes a bit set in body B's layer. Use bit flags (powers of 2) or the layer name editor to assign them.

query: Why is my RigidBody3D falling through the floor?
model_answer: Most floor-fall-through is caused by missing or mismatched CollisionShape3D. Ensure both the floor and RigidBody have non-overlapping CollisionShapes. Also check physics layers/masks and verify the floor is a StaticBody3D, not a plain MeshInstance3D.

query: How do I create a tilemap level in Godot 4?
model_answer: Add a TileMap node, create or import a TileSet, assign it to the TileMap. Use the TileMap editor panel to paint tiles. Layers within TileMap support z_index and y_sort. Access tile data at runtime via set_cell() and get_cell_tile_data().

query: How do I transition between scenes in Godot?
model_answer: Use get_tree().change_scene_to_file("res://my_scene.tscn") or change_scene_to_packed() with a preloaded PackedScene. For smooth transitions with loading screens use ResourceLoader.load_threaded_request() and poll with load_threaded_get_status().

query: What does yield do in GDScript and is it still available in Godot 4?
model_answer: yield was replaced by await in Godot 4. Use await signal_name or await get_tree().create_timer(1.0).timeout. The coroutine model is otherwise unchanged — awaiting a signal suspends the current function and resumes when the signal fires.

query: How do I implement object pooling in Godot?
model_answer: Maintain an Array of pre-instantiated nodes. On spawn, pop from the pool and make visible; on despawn, hide and push back. Use a custom pool manager node or autoload. Avoid queue_free() in high-frequency paths as it triggers GC.

query: What is a packed scene and how is it different from a regular scene?
model_answer: A PackedScene is the serialized in-memory representation of a scene file (.tscn). Calling load() on a .tscn returns a PackedScene; instantiate() creates a node tree from it. Use preload() or @export var for PackedScene references in scripts.

query: How do I use NavigationAgent3D for pathfinding?
model_answer: Add a NavigationAgent3D to your character, set target_position, then call get_next_path_position() in _physics_process to obtain the next waypoint. Move toward that position. Ensure a NavigationMesh bakes the walkable surface first via NavigationRegion3D.

query: How do I debug GDScript with breakpoints?
model_answer: Click the gutter in the GDScript editor to add a breakpoint. Run the project from the editor; execution pauses at the breakpoint and the Debugger panel shows the call stack, local variables, and watches. Use the Step Over / Step In buttons to advance.

query: Why is my shader not showing up on a MeshInstance3D?
model_answer: Spatial shaders require shader_type spatial; at the top. Ensure the ShaderMaterial is assigned to the MeshInstance3D's surface (not the mesh resource itself). Check for GLSL errors in the shader editor output — invalid syntax silently falls back to the default material.

query: What is the Node scene tree and how does parent-child relationship affect transforms?
model_answer: Nodes form a tree; each node's global_position is its local position plus the parent's global_position (recursively). Set position to move relative to the parent; global_position to move in world space. Detach from parent or use top_level = true to opt out of parent transform.

query: How do I export my Godot game to Android?
model_answer: Install the Android build template via Editor > Manage Export Templates. Configure an export preset in Project > Export, set your package name and keystore. Run adb or use USB debug mode. Minimum SDK version must be set ≥21 for Godot 4.

query: How does GDScript typing work and what are the benefits of typed variables?
model_answer: Declare typed variables with var x: int = 5 or use := for inference. Typed GDScript enables compile-time error checking, faster execution (avoids runtime type lookups), and better autocomplete. Use @warning_ignore or disable warnings per-project for mixed codebases.

query: How do I make an HTTP request to a REST API in Godot?
model_answer: Add an HTTPRequest node. Call request(url, headers, method, body). Connect the request_completed signal which gives you the response code and body as PackedByteArray. Parse JSON with JSON.parse_string(body.get_string_from_utf8()).

query: What is the difference between local and global scope in GDScript?
model_answer: Variables declared at the top of a script (class-level) are member variables accessible from all methods. Variables declared inside a function are local to that function's scope. GDScript does not have block scope — variables declared in if/for bodies are accessible for the rest of the function.

query: Why is my Camera2D not following the player smoothly?
model_answer: Enable position_smoothing_enabled on the Camera2D and set position_smoothing_speed. For drag-style following use drag_horizontal_enabled/drag_vertical_enabled and adjust the drag margins. Ensure the camera's limit bounds don't clamp movement prematurely.

query: How do I use GDExtension to add C++ code to Godot?
model_answer: GDExtension lets you write C++ (or other languages via bindings) that integrate as native Godot classes. Use godot-cpp to bind your classes, compile to a shared library, and describe it in a .gdextension file placed in your project.

query: What are groups in Godot and how do I use them?
model_answer: Groups are named collections of nodes. Add a node to a group with add_to_group("enemies") or via the Node panel in the editor. Call get_tree().get_nodes_in_group("enemies") to iterate all members. Use call_group() to invoke a method on every node in a group simultaneously.

query: How do I implement a day/night cycle with DirectionalLight3D?
model_answer: Rotate the DirectionalLight3D over time in _process() to simulate sun movement. Adjust the light's color and energy using a gradient keyed to the angle. Simultaneously update sky shader parameters or WorldEnvironment's sky properties for sky color transitions.

query: Why is my TileMap not showing collisions at runtime?
model_answer: Tile collisions must be defined in the TileSet editor per tile. Check that the physics layer is configured in TileSet (Project > Add Physics Layer). Ensure the TileMap's collision_layer and collision_mask bits are set. Enable debug collision shapes in Debug > Visible Collision Shapes.

query: How do I use tweens to animate properties in code?
model_answer: Create a Tween with create_tween(), then chain tween_property(node, "property", target_value, duration). Use .set_trans() and .set_ease() to control easing curves. Tweens are auto-freed when finished unless you call set_loops() or store a reference.

query: What is the best way to handle localization and translation in Godot?
model_answer: Add CSV or PO translation files in Project > Localization > Translations. Use tr("KEY") in code or the auto-translate property on Label nodes. Call TranslationServer.set_locale() to change language at runtime. Godot supports RTL text with TextServer.

query: How do I implement screen shake using a Camera2D?
model_answer: Modify the camera's offset property by adding random noise each frame, then decay it over time. Use a Timer or tween to control duration. Alternatively use offset with a Tween and RandomNumberGenerator.randf_range() for controlled random magnitude.

query: How does Godot handle multiple resolutions and screen scaling?
model_answer: Set the base resolution in Project Settings > Display > Window. Use Stretch Mode canvas_items or viewport, and Stretch Aspect keep_width, keep_height, or expand to control how the game fits different screen sizes. For pixel-perfect games use integer scaling.

query: How do I add global illumination with VoxelGI or LightmapGI?
model_answer: VoxelGI works dynamically at runtime — add the node, set its size, and call bake(). LightmapGI bakes at edit-time — mark meshes as bake_mode STATIC, add LightmapGI, and bake via the Scene menu. LightmapGI has better quality; VoxelGI supports dynamic objects.

query: Why is my AudioStreamPlayer not looping?
model_answer: Enable looping in the AudioStreamOGGVorbis or AudioStreamWAV resource itself (not on the player node). For AudioStreamWAV, set loop_mode to Forward. For OGG files, the loop metadata must be embedded by your DAW or set explicitly in the import settings.

query: What is the SceneTree and how do I use it to manage game state?
model_answer: SceneTree is the singleton managing the active node tree, physics/rendering loop, and scene transitions. Access it from any node with get_tree(). Use get_tree().paused = true to pause the game, change_scene_to_file() to switch scenes, and get_nodes_in_group() for group queries.

query: How do I use GDScript lambdas and Callable for event handling?
model_answer: Lambda syntax is func(args): body. Pass a lambda as a Callable: button.pressed.connect(func(): do_thing()). Callables can bind arguments with .bind(arg1, arg2). Use .call() to invoke manually.

query: How do I profile performance in Godot and identify bottlenecks?
model_answer: Open the Debugger > Profiler tab while running the game. Click Start to record. The profiler shows CPU time per function; the Monitors tab shows physics, GPU, and memory metrics. Use OS.get_ticks_usec() for manual micro-benchmarks in code.

query: What is the difference between Node2D position and global_position?
model_answer: position is relative to the parent node's origin. global_position is the absolute world-space position obtained by recursively applying parent transforms. Setting global_position lets you place a node in world coordinates regardless of the scene hierarchy.

query: How do I use Godot's built-in shader for post-processing effects?
model_answer: Attach a ShaderMaterial with shader_type canvas_item to a ColorRect covering the viewport (or use a SubViewport). Use the SCREEN_TEXTURE uniform (requires hint: screen_texture in the sampler declaration) to sample the full rendered frame for post-process effects.

query: Why does my Node get deleted unexpectedly during gameplay?
model_answer: Likely causes: queue_free() called elsewhere, parent scene unloaded, or a freed object's reference still held in a variable. Check is_instance_valid(node) before accessing. Signals from freed nodes become invalid — disconnect them or use CONNECT_ONE_SHOT. Use the Debugger's call stack on the crash to find the free site.

query: How do I use InputMap to support rebindable controls?
model_answer: Define actions in Project > Input Map. At runtime, remove existing events with InputMap.action_erase_events() and add new ones with InputMap.action_add_event(). Store the mapping in a config file with ConfigFile so it persists across sessions.

query: What is the best node to use for detecting line-of-sight between two objects?
model_answer: Use a RayCast2D (or RayCast3D for 3D). Set target_position to the vector toward the target, call force_raycast_update() if outside _physics_process, and check is_colliding(). The get_collider() method returns the first body hit.

query: How do I create a minimap for a 2D game?
model_answer: Use a SubViewport with its own Camera2D zoomed out to show the full level. Assign the SubViewport's texture to a TextureRect on a UI CanvasLayer. Keep the minimap camera positioned to follow the player using the same logic as the main camera.

query: What is the purpose of the _ready function in Godot?
model_answer: _ready is called once after the node and all its children have entered the scene tree. Use it for initialization that requires child nodes to exist — getting node references with $NodeName, connecting signals, or reading export variables set in the editor.

query: Why is my game running slowly on mobile but fine on desktop?
model_answer: Mobile GPUs are fill-rate limited. Reduce overdraw, use lower-resolution textures, and cut draw calls via MultiMeshInstance3D batching. Disable expensive features like SSAO, SSIL, and volumetric fog. Use Forward Mobile renderer instead of Forward+ for mobile targets.

query: How do I implement a dialogue system in Godot?
model_answer: Store dialogue in JSON or a Resource array. Create a DialogueManager autoload that tracks the current line and speaker. Use a CanvasLayer with a Panel, Label, and RichTextLabel for display. Advance lines on input, trigger branching via a Dictionary key lookup.

query: What are physics layers in Godot and how many can I have?
model_answer: Godot supports 32 physics layers per physics space (2D and 3D separately). Each layer is a bit in a 32-bit integer. Name layers in Project Settings > Layer Names > 2D Physics (or 3D Physics). Bodies only interact when the mask of one overlaps the layer of the other.

query: How do I use the MultiplayerAPI for an authoritative server in Godot?
model_answer: Use ENetMultiplayerPeer (or WebRTCMultiplayerPeer for browser) and call create_server() on the host. Use @rpc(any_peer, call_remote, reliable) annotations to define RPCs. Check multiplayer.is_server() to gate authoritative logic. Replicate state with MultiplayerSynchronizer.

query: Why is Input.get_axis returning 0 when I press my keys?
model_answer: Input.get_axis("negative_action", "positive_action") returns the difference between two action strengths. Ensure both action names exactly match entries in Project > Input Map (case-sensitive). Also check that the InputEvent's device is -1 (all devices) not a specific gamepad index if using keyboard.

query: How do I write unit tests for GDScript in Godot?
model_answer: Use GUT (Godot Unit Test) plugin or Godot's built-in assert() function. For CI-friendly testing, run with --headless --quit-after-n-frames in a test scene that calls assert() and prints results. GUT provides a more complete xUnit-style API with test discovery and reporting.

query: What is the purpose of export variables and how do they work?
model_answer: @export exposes a variable to the Inspector panel in the editor, letting designers set values without editing code. Supports all Variant types plus enums, arrays, and NodePath. Use @export_range, @export_color_no_alpha, etc. for constrained editors. Exported values are serialized into the .tscn/.tres file.

query: How do I detect when the player enters or exits an Area2D?
model_answer: Connect the Area2D's body_entered and body_exited signals to methods on your Area2D node. The signals pass the body node as the first argument. For other Area2D overlaps use area_entered/area_exited. Ensure both nodes have matching collision layer/mask bits set.
